description: Strategic assumption validation framework — concrete tests for ClawHQ's 3 highest-risk assumptions

reviewCadence.process: Review each assumption against latest data. Update 'latestData' fields. If any assumption hits FAIL threshold, trigger the documented response. Record review in 'reviewLog'.

assumptions.name: Addressable Demand
assumptions.assumption: OpenClaw's 2M+ users includes meaningful demand for a sovereign deployment tool
assumptions.rationale: 2M MAU sounds large, but each filter (self-hosted, technical, security-conscious, willing to use CLI) cuts 80-90%. The actual addressable market may be tiny.

test.method: Measure GitHub clone → clawhq install conversion in first 1,000 unique visitors
test.dataSource: GitHub repo analytics (clones, unique visitors) + install telemetry (curl script hit count)
trackingSetup: Enable GitHub repo traffic analytics (Settings → Traffic)
Add unique visitor counter to curl install script (privacy-respecting: count only, no PII)
Track: unique visitors, unique clones, install script executions, successful installs
Funnel: visitor → clone → install attempt → successful install → 7-day retention
test.passCriteria: >5% clone-to-install conversion (50+ installs from first 1,000 unique visitors)
test.failResponse: Pivot to authority play: content, upstream contribution, open-source reference implementation. Reassess product thesis.
test.ambiguousRange: 2-5% conversion — inconclusive, extend test to 2,000 visitors before deciding

assumptions.name: Willingness to Pay
assumptions.assumption: Sovereignty audience will pay for monitoring that enhances (not compromises) sovereignty
assumptions.rationale: Sentinel is the first revenue experiment. If sovereign users won't pay for sovereign monitoring, the revenue model needs rethinking.

test.method: Sentinel landing page with pricing, measure signups
test.dataSource: Landing page analytics (visits, signup form submissions)
trackingSetup: Create Sentinel landing page at clawhq.com/sentinel (or subdomain)
Include: value proposition, pricing ($X/mo), signup form (email + 'I'd pay for this' commitment)
Track: page visits, time on page, signup form submissions, email confirmations
Funnel: landing page visit → read pricing → submit signup → confirm email
test.failCriteria: <3 signups — monitoring isn't the revenue model
test.failResponse: Explore alternative revenue: premium blueprints, enterprise fleet management, or marketplace commission model.
test.ambiguousRange: 3-9 signups — weak signal, extend test 30 days and add direct user interviews

assumptions.name: Foundation Absorption Risk
assumptions.assumption: OpenClaw foundation won't ship guided configuration tooling within 12 months
assumptions.rationale: Steinberger joined OpenAI Feb 2026, project at foundation. If the foundation ships 'openclaw configure --guided', ClawHQ's core differentiator erodes. This is an existential risk.

test.method: Monitor OpenClaw GitHub for configuration-related PRs, RFCs, and roadmap items
test.dataSource: OpenClaw GitHub repository (PRs, issues, RFCs, roadmap discussions)
trackingSetup: Set up GitHub watch on OpenClaw repo with notifications for PRs/issues
Monitor OpenClaw Discord #development channel for roadmap discussions
Check OpenClaw foundation meeting notes (if public) monthly
Track: number of config-related PRs, RFC proposals, roadmap mentions
test.passCriteria: 0-1 config-related PRs in 6 months — foundation is not prioritizing this
test.failCriteria: 2+ PRs targeting guided configuration — assumption under attack
test.failResponse: Accelerate blueprint spec publication. Propose upstream collaboration to position ClawHQ as the reference implementation. Shift from competing with foundation to partnering.
test.ambiguousRange: 1 PR with no follow-up — monitor closely, increase review to biweekly
